Execute this bridled pace work - Agent B portion:

**Context:** Heat ‚Ç£AF (jjk-post-alpha-polish), Pace ‚Ç¢AFAAB (gallops-commit-routine)

**Your task:** Document the `jjdr_persist` routine in JJD-GallopsData.adoc following existing routine patterns.

**Detailed steps:**

1. Read `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJD-GallopsData.adoc` to understand existing patterns for:
   - The mapping section (attribute references)
   - The Routines section format (existing routines like jjdr_load, jjdr_save)

2. Add to the mapping section:
   - `:jjdr_persist:` attribute reference (follow existing pattern)

3. Add to the Routines section:
   - Document `jjdr_persist` routine following the existing pattern
   - Description: Persists gallops state and commits gallops+paddock files
   - Signature: `jjri_persist(lock, gallops, file, firemark, message, size_limit) -> Result<commit_hash>`
   - Note which commands use it (nominate, slate, rail, tally, draft, furlough)
   - Note which commands do NOT use it (retire - custom trophy logic, chalk - empty commits only)

**Follow MCM (Meta Concept Model) patterns:**
- Attribute reference format: `:prefix_term: <<anchor,Display Text>>`
- Anchor format: `[[prefix_term]]`

**Constraint:** Schema-stable ‚Äî documentation only, matching the implementation being done in parallel.

   130‚Üí
   131‚Üí== Overview
   132‚Üí
   133‚ÜíThe
   134‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   135‚Üíis the persistent data store for Job Jockey.
   136‚ÜíIt tracks
   137‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_s}
   138‚Üí(bounded initiatives) and their constituent
   139‚Üí{jjdpr_pace_s}
   140‚Üí(discrete actions).
   141‚ÜíEach
   142‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}
   143‚Üímaintains a history of
   144‚Üí{jjdkr_tack_s}
   145‚Üí(plan refinements).
   146‚Üí
   147‚Üí== Design Principles
   148‚Üí
   149‚Üí=== Exclusive JSON Ownership
   150‚Üí
   151‚ÜíAll access to the
   152‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   153‚Üígoes through {jjdx_cli}, the Job Jockey subcommand of the Voce Viva executor.
   154‚ÜíNo jq or direct JSON manipulation ever touches this data.
   155‚ÜíThis is an absolute architectural constraint, not a preference.
   156‚Üí
   157‚ÜíThe rationale: writing reliable bash with jq pipelines requires fighting LLM training data.
   158‚ÜíThe internet is full of bad bash.
   159‚ÜíRust replaces fragile jq invocations with compiler-enforced correctness.
   160‚ÜíThe compiler is tireless; humans are not.
   161‚Üí
   162‚Üí=== Git as Journal
   163‚Üí
   164‚ÜíThe
   165‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   166‚Üíremains JSON specifically so git can serve as the journalling layer.
   167‚ÜíEvery mutation creates a diffable commit.
   168‚ÜíGit owns the complete history; we need not implement our own audit trail.
   169‚Üí
   170‚ÜíThe
   171‚Üí{jjdkr_tack}
   172‚Üíhistory within each
   173‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}
   174‚Üíexists for convenient postmortem analysis.
   175‚ÜíGit has the full history, but
   176‚Üí{jjdkr_tack_s}
   177‚Üílet us see plan evolution without spelunking commits.
   178‚Üí
   179‚Üí=== Diff-Friendly Mutations
   180‚Üí
   181‚ÜíNew
   182‚Üí{jjdkr_tack_s}
   183‚Üíprepend to position 0 rather than append.
   184‚ÜíThis produces cleaner git diffs: prepending adds a line with trailing comma, while appending would modify the previous last line to add a comma.
   185‚ÜíSmall discipline, large cumulative benefit.
   186‚Üí
   187‚ÜíThe
   188‚Üí{jjdhm_order}
   189‚Üíarray contains full
   190‚Üí{jjdt_coronet_s}.
   191‚ÜíEach
   192‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   193‚Üíembeds its parent
   194‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   195‚Üí(redundant within a single
   196‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}).
   197‚ÜíThis redundancy is cheap and enables grep, copy-paste, and self-documenting JSON.
   198‚Üí
   199‚Üí=== Crash-Safe Architecture
   200‚Üí
   201‚ÜíBash handles locking via `git update-ref`.
   202‚Üí{jjdx_cli} owns all
   203‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   204‚Üífile access - reading, transforming, and writing.
   205‚Üí{jjdx_cli} implements atomic writes internally (write to temp file, then rename).
   206‚ÜíIf {jjdx_cli} crashes mid-transform, no partial writes occur.
   207‚Üí
   208‚ÜíThe pattern:
   209‚Üí[source,bash]
   210‚Üí----
   211‚Üílock_acquire
   212‚Üívvx jjx gallops nominate --file gallops.json "my-heat" "260111" || { lock_release; die; }
   213‚Üílock_release
   214‚Üí----
   215‚Üí
   216‚ÜíThis keeps bash simple (just locking and invocation) while {jjdx_cli} owns all JSON file operations.
   217‚Üí
   218‚Üí=== Deterministic Serialization
   219‚Üí
   220‚ÜíImplementations MUST serialize JSON object keys in deterministic order to minimize diff churn.
   221‚ÜíFor `heats` and `paces` objects, use sorted key order (e.g., Rust `BTreeMap` instead of `HashMap`).
   222‚ÜíThis ensures that reordering paces via
   223‚Üí{jjdo_rail}
   224‚Üíproduces minimal diffs ‚Äî only the `order` array changes, not the entire `paces` object.
   225‚Üí
   226‚Üí=== Identity Encoding
   227‚Üí
   228‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   229‚Üí(`‚Ç£` prefix) and
   230‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   231‚Üí(`‚Ç¢` prefix) are the canonical identities used in JSON keys, CLI arguments, and display.
   232‚ÜíThe base64 encoding provides compact, URL-safe, shell-safe strings.
   233‚ÜíInternally, Rust may decode to integers for arithmetic, but the string form is authoritative.
   234‚Üí
   235‚Üí=== Horse Racing Vocabulary
   236‚Üí
   237‚ÜíJob Jockey uses horse racing metaphors throughout.
   238‚ÜíThis is intentional: vivid domain vocabulary helps humans hold complex systems in working memory.
   239‚Üí
   240‚Üí* {jjdhr_heat} - a bounded initiative (like a race)
   241‚Üí* {jjdpr_pace} - a discrete action (the rhythm of the race)
   242‚Üí* {jjdkr_tack} - plan refinement (equipment; also "tacking" = adjusting course)
   243‚Üí* {jjdt_firemark} - heat identity, `‚Ç£`-prefixed (hot-iron brand marking ownership)
   244‚Üí* {jjdt_coronet} - pace identity, `‚Ç¢`-prefixed (marking at the hoof line)
   245‚Üí* {jjdt_silks} - display name (jockey's colors)
   246‚Üí* {jjdgr_gallops} - the training grounds (where real work happens before race day)
   247‚Üí
   248‚Üí== Types
   249‚Üí
   250‚Üí[[jjdt_firemark]]
   251‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_string‚üß
   252‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}::
   253‚ÜíThe identity of a
   254‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}.
   255‚ÜíFormat: `‚Ç£` + 2 base64 characters (e.g., `‚Ç£AB`).
   256‚ÜíThe 2 base64 characters encode an integer 0-4095 (2^12).
   257‚ÜíUsed as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
   258‚ÜíInput may omit the `‚Ç£` prefix; output always includes it.
   259‚Üí
   260‚Üí[[jjdt_coronet]]
   261‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_string‚üß
   262‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}::
   263‚ÜíThe identity of a
   264‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}.
   265‚ÜíFormat: `‚Ç¢` + 5 base64 characters (e.g., `‚Ç¢ABCDE`).
   266‚ÜíGlobally unique: the first 2 base64 characters are the parent
   267‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_p}
   268‚Üíencoded identity; the remaining 3 encode the pace index (0-262143, 2^18).
   269‚ÜíUsed as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
   270‚ÜíInput may omit the `‚Ç¢` prefix; output always includes it.
   271‚Üí+
   272‚Üí*Reassignment:* When a
   273‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}
   274‚Üíis moved between
   275‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_s}
   276‚Üívia
   277‚Üí{jjdo_draft},
   278‚Üíthe old
   279‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   280‚Üíbecomes invalid and a new one is assigned using the destination
   281‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_p}
   282‚Üí{jjdhm_seed}.
   283‚ÜíThe new
   284‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   285‚Üíembeds the destination
   286‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}.
   287‚Üí{jjdkr_tack}
   288‚Üíhistory is preserved, with a note recording the original
   289‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}.
   290‚Üí
   291‚Üí[[jjdt_silks]]
   292‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_string‚üß
   293‚Üí{jjdt_silks}::
   294‚ÜíA kebab-case display name.
   295‚ÜíUsed for human-readable identification of
   296‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_s}
   297‚Üíand
   298‚Üí{jjdpr_pace_s}.
   299‚ÜíPattern: `[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*`
   300‚Üí
   301‚Üí== Records
   302‚Üí
   303‚Üí=== {jjdgr_gallops}
   304‚Üí
   305‚Üí[[jjdgr_gallops]]
   306‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_record_json‚üß
   307‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}::
   308‚ÜíThe root JSON structure containing all Job Jockey data.
   309‚ÜíSingleton record persisted at `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.
   310‚Üí
   311‚Üí==== Members
   312‚Üí
   313‚Üí* [[jjdgm_seed]]
   314‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   315‚Üí{jjdgm_seed}
   316‚Üíis the next
   317‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   318‚Üíto allocate, stored as 2 base64 characters.
   319‚ÜíIncremented by
   320‚Üí{jjdo_nominate}.
   321‚Üí
   322‚Üí=== {jjdhr_heat}
   323‚Üí
   324‚Üí[[jjdhr_heat]]
   325‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_record_json‚üß
   326‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}::
   327‚ÜíA bounded initiative with coherent goals.
   328‚ÜíContains zero or more
   329‚Üí{jjdpr_pace_s}
   330‚Üírepresenting discrete actions.
   331‚ÜíKeyed in JSON by
   332‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}.
   333‚Üí
   334‚Üí==== Members
   335‚Üí
   336‚Üí* [[jjdhm_firemark]]
   337‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   338‚Üí{jjdhm_firemark}
   339‚Üíis the
   340‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_p}
   341‚Üíprimary key.
   342‚ÜíType:
   343‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}.
   344‚ÜíImplicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.
   345‚Üí
   346‚Üí* [[jjdhm_silks]]
   347‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   348‚Üí{jjdhm_silks}
   349‚Üíis the
   350‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_p}
   351‚Üídisplay name.
   352‚ÜíType:
   353‚Üí{jjdt_silks}.
   354‚Üí
   355‚Üí* [[jjdhm_created]]
   356‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   357‚Üí{jjdhm_created}
   358‚Üírecords when the
   359‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
   360‚Üíwas nominated.
   361‚ÜíFormat: YYMMDD.
   362‚Üí
   363‚Üí* [[jjdhm_status]]
   364‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   365‚Üí{jjdhm_status}
   366‚Üíindicates the
   367‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_p}
   368‚Üílifecycle state.
   369‚ÜíValues:
   370‚Üí{jjdhe_racing},
   371‚Üí{jjdhe_stabled},
   372‚Üíor
   373‚Üí{jjdhe_retired}.
   374‚Üí
   375‚Üí* [[jjdhm_order]]
   376‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_repeated‚üß
   377‚Üí{jjdhm_order}
   378‚Üílists
   379‚Üí{jjdt_coronet_s}
   380‚Üíin execution sequence.
   381‚ÜíMay be reordered via
   382‚Üí{jjdo_rail}.
   383‚Üí
   384‚Üí* [[jjdhm_seed]]
   385‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   386‚Üí{jjdhm_seed}
   387‚Üíis the next
   388‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   389‚Üíindex to allocate, stored as 3 base64 characters.
   390‚ÜíIncremented by
   391‚Üí{jjdo_slate}.
   392‚Üí
   393‚Üí* [[jjdhm_paddock]]
   394‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   395‚Üí{jjdhm_paddock}
   396‚Üíis the path to the paddock file containing
   397‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
   398‚Üícontext and background.
   399‚ÜíFormat: `.claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md` where XX is the base64-encoded
   400‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   401‚Üí(without `‚Ç£` prefix).
   402‚ÜíCreated by
   403‚Üí{jjdo_nominate}.
   404‚Üí
   405‚Üí==== Status Values
   406‚Üí
   407‚Üí[[jjdhe_racing]]
   408‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_enum_value‚üß
   409‚Üí{jjdhe_racing}::
   410‚ÜíThe
   411‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
   412‚Üíis actively being worked.
   413‚Üí
   414‚Üí[[jjdhe_stabled]]
   415‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_enum_value‚üß
   416‚Üí{jjdhe_stabled}::
   417‚ÜíThe
   418‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}

   577‚Üí== Serialization
   578‚Üí
   579‚Üí[[jjdz_charset]]
   580‚Üí{jjdz_charset}::
   581‚ÜíThe 64-character alphabet for identity encoding.
   582‚ÜíUses URL-safe base64 (RFC 4648 ¬ß5):
   583‚Üí`ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789-_`
   584‚ÜíPosition in charset determines numeric value (A=0, B=1, ..., `-`=62, `_`=63).
   585‚ÜíThis alphabet is safe for JSON keys, URLs, shell, git refs, and filenames.
   586‚ÜíMatches Rust `base64::engine::general_purpose::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD`.
   587‚Üí
   588‚Üí[[jjdz_encoding]]
   589‚Üí{jjdz_encoding}::
   590‚ÜíThe algorithm for encoding
   591‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   592‚Üíand
   593‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   594‚Üívalues.
   595‚Üí+
   596‚Üí*{jjdt_firemark} (Heat identity):*
   597‚Üí+
   598‚Üí. Format: `‚Ç£` + 2 base64 characters
   599‚Üí. Given integer H (0-4095): encode as `‚Ç£` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]`
   600‚Üí. To decode `‚Ç£XY`: H = pos(X) * 64 + pos(Y)
   601‚Üí+
   602‚Üí*{jjdt_coronet} (Pace identity):*
   603‚Üí+
   604‚Üí. Format: `‚Ç¢` + 5 base64 characters
   605‚Üí. First 2 characters encode the parent Heat (same as {jjdt_firemark} without prefix)
   606‚Üí. Last 3 characters encode the pace index P (0-262143)
   607‚Üí. Given Heat H and pace index P: encode as `‚Ç¢` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]` + `charset[P/4096]` + `charset[(P/64)%64]` + `charset[P%64]`
   608‚Üí. To decode `‚Ç¢XYZAB`: H from XY, P = pos(Z) * 4096 + pos(A) * 64 + pos(B)
   609‚Üí+
   610‚Üí*Input flexibility:*
   611‚Üí+
   612‚ÜíCLI commands accept identities with or without prefix.
   613‚ÜíLength determines type: 2 base64 chars = {jjdt_firemark}, 5 base64 chars = {jjdt_coronet}.
   614‚ÜíOutput always includes the prefix (`‚Ç£` or `‚Ç¢`).

   802‚Üí== CLI
   803‚Üí
   804‚Üí[[jjdx_vvx]]
   805‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cli_program‚üß
   806‚Üí{jjdx_vvx}::
   807‚ÜíThe Voce Viva executor.
   808‚ÜíLocal reference to the VVK-defined CLI program that hosts Job Jockey.
   809‚Üí
   810‚Üí[[jjdx_cli]]
   811‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cli_command_group‚üß
   812‚Üí{jjdx_cli}::
   813‚ÜíThe Job Jockey command group within
   814‚Üí{jjdx_vvx}.
   815‚ÜíInvoked as `vvx jjx`.
   816‚ÜíAll operations are subcommands of
   817‚Üí{jjdx_cli}.
   818‚ÜíInherits Unix CLI semantics: stdout for results, stderr for errors, exit 0 for success, non-zero for failure.
   819‚Üí
   820‚Üí== Commit Message Architecture
   821‚Üí
   822‚ÜíAll JJ commits include an action code that serves multiple purposes:
   823‚Üí- **Parsing steeplechase history**: Distinguishes between different operation types
   824‚Üí- **Semantic clarity**: Indicates the nature and context of each commit
   825‚Üí- **Consistency**: Applies uniformly across pace-level and heat-level operations
   826‚Üí
   827‚Üí== Routines
   828‚Üí
   829‚ÜíRoutines are internal reusable procedures invoked by operations.
   830‚ÜíThey encapsulate common logic for consistent behavior across the codebase.
   831‚Üí
   832‚Üí[[jjdr_load]]
   833‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axo_routine‚üß
   834‚Üí=== {jjdr_load}
   835‚Üí
   836‚ÜíLoad and validate the
   837‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   838‚Üífile.
   839‚ÜíEvery operation that reads
   840‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   841‚Üíinvokes this routine.
   842‚ÜíReturns a validated
   843‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   844‚Üíonly after all checks pass.
   845‚Üí
   846‚Üí{jjds_arguments}
   847‚Üí
   848‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
   849‚Üí* {jjda_file}
   850‚Üí
   851‚Üí{jjds_behavior}
   852‚Üí
   853‚Üí. Read file contents as raw bytes
   854‚Üí. Deserialize bytes into
   855‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   856‚Üístructure
   857‚Üí. Reserialize
   858‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   859‚Üístructure to JSON string
   860‚Üí. Compare reserialized bytes against original bytes
   861‚Üí. If different:
   862‚Üí{axc_fatal}
   863‚Üíwith diagnostic showing first difference location
   864‚Üí. Validate semantic constraints (timestamps, kebab-case silks, invariants)
   865‚Üí. If validation fails:
   866‚Üí{axc_fatal}
   867‚Üíwith validation errors
   868‚Üí. Return validated
   869‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   870‚Üí
   871‚Üí{jjds_stdout} None (returns structure to caller)
   872‚Üí
   873‚Üí{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero on validation failure or I/O error.
   874‚Üí
   875‚Üí*Architectural constraint:*
   876‚Üí{jjdr_load}
   877‚Üíis the ONLY way to obtain a
   878‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   879‚Üífrom disk.
   880‚ÜíThis makes validation bypass architecturally impossible.
   881‚ÜíThe round-trip check (steps 3-5) ensures the file is in canonical form.
   882‚ÜíThe semantic validation (steps 6-7) ensures business rules are satisfied.
   883‚Üí
   884‚Üí[[jjdr_save]]
   885‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axo_routine‚üß
   886‚Üí=== {jjdr_save}
   887‚Üí
   888‚ÜíPersist
   889‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   890‚Üíto disk with validation.
   891‚ÜíEvery operation that writes
   892‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   893‚Üíinvokes this routine.
   894‚ÜíValidates output by loading it back before finalizing.
   895‚Üí
   896‚Üí{jjds_arguments}
   897‚Üí
   898‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
   899‚Üí*
   900‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   901‚Üí(in-memory structure to persist)
   902‚Üí
   903‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
   904‚Üí* {jjda_file}
   905‚Üí
   906‚Üí{jjds_behavior}
   907‚Üí
   908‚Üí. Serialize
   909‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   910‚Üíto canonical JSON string
   911‚Üí. Write JSON to temporary file (atomic write preparation)
   912‚Üí. Call
   913‚Üí{jjdr_load}
   914‚Üíon temporary file to validate what was written
   915‚Üí. If
   916‚Üí{jjdr_load}
   917‚Üífails: delete temporary file,
   918‚Üí{axc_fatal}
   919‚Üíwith error
   920‚Üí. Rename temporary file to final path (atomic commit)
   921‚Üí
   922‚Üí{jjds_stdout} None
   923‚Üí
   924‚Üí{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero on validation failure or I/O error.
   925‚Üí
   926‚Üí*Design rationale:*
   927‚Üí{jjdr_save}
   928‚Üíreuses
   929‚Üí{jjdr_load}
   930‚Üífor validation, ensuring a single validation code path for both reading and writing.
   931‚ÜíIf an operation produces invalid output, the save fails before corrupting the file.
   932‚Üí
   933‚Üí== Operations
   934‚Üí
   935‚ÜíOperations are implemented as {jjdx_cli} subcommands.
   936‚Üí{jjdx_cli} owns all
   937‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   938‚Üífile access through
   939‚Üí{jjdr_load}
   940‚Üíand
   941‚Üí{jjdr_save}.
   942‚ÜíBash handles locking via `git update-ref` and invokes {jjdx_cli} with
   943‚Üí{jjda_file}.

  1192‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand‚üß
  1193‚Üí==== {jjdo_rail}

  1201‚ÜíSupports two modes: *order mode* replaces the entire sequence; *move mode* relocates a single
  1202‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}.

  1212‚Üí----

  1214‚Üí{jjds_arguments}
  1215‚Üí
  1216‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_optional axd_defaulted‚üß

  1228‚Üí{jjdt_coronet_s}

  1240‚Üí{jjda_before},
  1241‚Üí{jjda_after},
  1242‚Üí{jjda_first},
  1243‚Üíor
  1244‚Üí{jjda_last}

  1246‚Üí*Mode detection:* If
  1247‚Üí{jjda_move}

  1249‚Üí
  1250‚Üí{jjds_stdout} Updated
  1251‚Üí{jjdhm_order}
  1252‚Üíarray, one
  1253‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
  1254‚Üíper line, in new sequence.
  1255‚Üí
  1256‚Üí{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1257‚Üí
  1258‚Üí{jjds_behavior}
  1259‚Üí
  1260‚Üí*Common steps:*
  1261‚Üí
  1262‚Üí.
  1263‚Üí{jjdr_load}
  1264‚Üí{jjda_file};
  1265‚Üíon failure, exit immediately with
  1266‚Üí{jjdr_load}
  1267‚Üíerror status
  1268‚Üí. Verify
  1269‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
  1270‚Üíexists with given
  1271‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
  1272‚Üí
  1273‚Üí*Order mode:*

  1275‚Üí. Parse positional arguments into array of

  1287‚Üí`paces` object

  1295‚Üí.
  1296‚Üí{jjdr_save}
  1297‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
  1298‚Üíto
  1299‚Üí{jjda_file}
  1300‚Üí. Output new
  1301‚Üí{jjdhm_order},
  1302‚Üíone
  1303‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}

  1306‚Üí*Move mode:*

  1309‚Üí{jjda_move}
  1310‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}

  1329‚Üí.. If

  1338‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}

  1341‚Üíinsert immediately after target

  1343‚Üí.
  1344‚Üí{jjdr_save}
  1345‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
  1346‚Üíto
  1347‚Üí{jjda_file}
  1348‚Üí. Output new

  1350‚Üíone
  1351‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
  1352‚Üíper line

  2249‚Üí=== Steeplechase Operations
  2250‚Üí
  2251‚ÜíThe steeplechase is Job Jockey's session history, stored in git commits rather than the
  2252‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
  2253‚ÜíJSON.
  2254‚ÜíCommit messages follow patterns that
  2255‚Üí{jjdo_rein}
  2256‚Üícan parse to reconstruct session history.
  2257‚Üí
  2258‚Üí==== Commit Message Patterns
  2259‚Üí
  2260‚ÜíAll JJ commits use the format: `jjb:HALLMARK:IDENTITY:ACTION: message`
  2261‚Üí
  2262‚Üí* `jjb` ‚Äî JJ brand prefix (literal)
  2263‚Üí* `HALLMARK` ‚Äî Version identifier. Format: NNNN (installed) or NNNN-xxxxxxx (dev). Source: `.vvk/vvbf_brand.json` (installed) or max hallmark from `Tools/vok/vov_veiled/vovr_registry.json` + git HEAD (Kit Forge)
  2264‚Üí* `IDENTITY` ‚Äî ‚Ç¢CORONET for pace-level, ‚Ç£FIREMARK for heat-level
  2265‚Üí* `ACTION` ‚Äî single-letter code (required for all commits)
  2266‚Üí
  2267‚Üí*Standard notch* (created by {jjdo_notch}):
  2268‚Üí----
  2269‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç¢XXXXX:n: commit message
  2270‚Üí
  2271‚ÜíCo-Authored-By: ...
  2272‚Üí----
  2273‚Üí
  2274‚ÜíStandard notch commits use `n` to denote routine progress tracking.
  2275‚Üí
  2276‚Üí*Pace-level chalk* (created by {jjdo_chalk}):
  2277‚Üí----
  2278‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç¢XXXXX:A: description
  2279‚Üí
  2280‚ÜíCo-Authored-By: ...
  2281‚Üí----
  2282‚Üí
  2283‚ÜíPace-level action codes:
  2284‚Üí
  2285‚Üí* `n` ‚Äî Notch: standard commit
  2286‚Üí* `A` ‚Äî APPROACH: proposed approach before work begins
  2287‚Üí* `W` ‚Äî WRAP: pace completion summary
  2288‚Üí* `F` ‚Äî FLY: autonomous execution began (bridled pace)
  2289‚Üí* `d` ‚Äî discussion: significant decision (lowercase, can also be heat-level)
  2290‚Üí
  2291‚Üí*Heat-level operations*:
  2292‚Üí----
  2293‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç£XX:N: heat-silks
  2294‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç£XX:S: pace-silks
  2295‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç£XX:r: reordered
  2296‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç£XX:T: pace-silks
  2297‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç£XX:D: old-coronet ‚Üí new-coronet
  2298‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç£XX:R: heat-silks
  2299‚Üí----
  2300‚Üí
  2301‚ÜíHeat-level action codes:
  2302‚Üí
  2303‚Üí* `N` ‚Äî Nominate: create new heat
  2304‚Üí* `S` ‚Äî Slate: add new pace
  2305‚Üí* `r` ‚Äî rail: reorder paces (lowercase)
  2306‚Üí* `T` ‚Äî Tally: add tack to pace
  2307‚Üí* `D` ‚Äî Draft: move pace between heats (uppercase, rare)
  2308‚Üí* `R` ‚Äî Retire: archive heat (uppercase)
  2309‚Üí
  2310‚Üí[[jjdo_rein]]
  2311‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand‚üß
  2312‚Üí==== {jjdo_rein}
  2313‚Üí
  2314‚ÜíParse git history for steeplechase entries belonging to a
  2315‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}.
  2316‚Üí
  2317‚Üí{jjds_arguments}
  2318‚Üí
  2319‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
  2320‚Üí* {jjdt_firemark}
  2321‚Üí(positional) ‚Äî target
  2322‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
  2323‚Üí
  2324‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_optional‚üß
  2325‚Üí* `--limit` ‚Äî maximum entries to return (default: 50)
  2326‚Üí
  2327‚Üí{jjds_stdout} JSON array of steeplechase entries:
  2328‚Üí[source,json]
  2329‚Üí----
  2330‚Üí[
  2331‚Üí  {
  2332‚Üí    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
  2333‚Üí    "commit": "abc123ef",
  2334‚Üí    "hallmark": "1009",
  2335‚Üí    "coronet": "‚Ç¢XXXXX",
  2336‚Üí    "action": "n",
  2337‚Üí    "subject": "commit message"
  2338‚Üí  },
  2339‚Üí  {
  2340‚Üí    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
  2341‚Üí    "commit": "def456ab",
  2342‚Üí    "hallmark": "1009-abc1234",
  2343‚Üí    "coronet": "‚Ç¢XXXXX",
  2344‚Üí    "action": "A",
  2345‚Üí    "subject": "description"
  2346‚Üí  },
  2347‚Üí  {
  2348‚Üí    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
  2349‚Üí    "hallmark": "1009",
  2350‚Üí    "action": "N",
  2351‚Üí    "subject": "heat-silks"
  2352‚Üí  }
  2353‚Üí]
  2354‚Üí----
  2355‚ÜíField `hallmark` is always present.
  2356‚ÜíField `coronet` is present for pace-level entries, absent for heat-level operations.
  2357‚ÜíField `action` is present for all commits, including standard notch commits (`n`).
  2358‚ÜíEntries ordered newest first.
  2359‚Üí
  2360‚Üí{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  2361‚Üí
  2362‚Üí{jjds_behavior}
  2363‚Üí
  2364‚Üí. Run `git log --all --extended-regexp --grep="^jjb:[^:]+:(‚Ç£XX|‚Ç¢XX)" --format="%ai%x09%s"`
  2365‚Üí  (skips hallmark field; filtering is by identity)
  2366‚Üí. Parse each line: extract timestamp, hallmark, identity, action, and message
  2367‚Üí. For pace-level (‚Ç¢CORONET): verify coronet belongs to target heat
  2368‚Üí. For heat-level (‚Ç£FIREMARK): verify firemark matches target heat
  2369‚Üí. Build JSON array, limit to requested count
  2370‚Üí. Output JSON
  2371‚Üí

  2373‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axi_cli_subcommand‚üß
  2374‚Üí==== {jjdo_notch}
  2375‚Üí
  2376‚ÜíCreate a JJ-aware git commit with heat/pace context prefix.
  2377‚Üí
  2378‚Üí{jjds_arguments}
  2379‚Üí
  2380‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
  2381‚Üí* {jjdt_coronet}
  2382‚Üí(positional) ‚Äî active pace identity (embeds parent heat)
  2383‚Üí
  2384‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_optional‚üß

  2386‚ÜíUse when the pace spec justifies legitimate large commits.
  2387‚Üí
  2388‚Üí{jjds_stdout} Commit hash
  2389‚Üí
  2390‚Üí{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  2391‚Üí
  2392‚Üí{jjds_behavior}
  2393‚Üí
  2394‚Üí. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
